value.body:
## Context

Agents should work outside the user's primary worktree. The repo already has `@cycle/git-worktrees` direction, but ticket assignment needs a product-level integration.

## Outcome

Assigning implementation work creates or reuses a dedicated read-write worktree with setup profiles, safe branch naming, durable lifecycle state, and cleanup/reconciliation.

## Acceptance criteria

- Ticket assignment can request a managed worktree and receive the path, branch name, and lifecycle id.
- Worktree setup supports repository-specific commands and failure reporting before the agent starts.
- Dirty or failed worktrees are retained or backed up according to policy rather than deleted silently.
- Successful runs publish a branch and mark worktree cleanup/retention state.
- Desktop/API/agent runtime all use `@cycle/git-worktrees` rather than ad hoc worktree logic.
- Tests cover creation, setup failure, cancellation, finalization, push failure, and cleanup.

frontmatter.title: Provision isolated read-write worktrees for assigned agent tickets

value.title: Provision isolated read-write worktrees for assigned agent tickets
